name: Vilasouto Reservoir Trail
name_original: Embalse Vilasouto – circular por la cuenca del río Mao
description: Hike the moderate 9.0-mile Vilasouto Reservoir Trail, gaining 795 feet in under 4 hours, exploring churches and the reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around O Incio offers varied terrain, situated between the Lemos Valley and the Sierra de Courel. The region features extensive deciduous forests, gentle mountains, and green valleys with numerous streams. A notable natural feature is the Vilasouto Reservoir, surrounded by hills. This area is also part of the "Reserva da Biosfera Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e Courel," highlighting its ecological significance. Best hiking trails around O Incio The most popular hiking route is Vilasouto Reservoir Trail, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 59 minutes to complete. This route explores the surroundings of the emblematic Vilasouto Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Main Street, Sarria – Sculpture loop from A Devesa do Porto, a difficult 15.8 miles (25.4 km) path. This route features a loop that includes sculptures and offers views of the landscape near Sarria. Local hikers also love the Church of Saint James in Renche – Outeiro Village loop from Fontearcuda, a 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail leading through rural villages and past historical sites, often completed in about 7 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around O Incio is defined by extensive deciduous forests, the Vilasouto Reservoir, and varied topography blending valley and mountainous landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in O Incio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore O Incio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in O Incio?
A: O Incio offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking routes. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in O Incio?
A: The terrain in O Incio is quite varied, situated between the Lemos Valley and the Sierra de Courel. You'll encounter extensive deciduous forests, gentle mountains, green valleys, and numerous streams. The area is also home to the emblematic Vilasouto Reservoir, offering picturesque walks along its shores.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for families or beginners in O Incio?
A: Yes, O Incio has several easy hiking routes perfect for families or those new to hiking. There are 13 easy trails available, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without strenuous effort. Many of these routes pass through serene forests and along tranquil waters.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see while hiking in O Incio?
A: While hiking in O Incio, you can discover several interesting natural and historical landmarks. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/424987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 800 years old. The Vilasouto Reservoir itself is a significant natural feature, and you might also encounter petroglyphs along its shores. The region is part of the "Reserva da Biosfera Ribeira Sacra e Serras do Oribio e Courel," highlighting its ecological value.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in O Incio?
A: Many of the trails in O Incio are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint James in Renche – Outeiro Village loop from Fontearcuda&lt;/a&gt; takes you through rural villages and past historical sites, offering a comprehensive experience of the local landscape.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in O Incio?
A: The hiking trails in O Incio are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved natural environments, the varied topography, and the peaceful atmosphere of the deciduous forests and reservoir areas.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in O Incio?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in O Incio. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste. The natural, open spaces are ideal for enjoying a walk with your canine companion.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Yes, O Incio is rich in history. Some routes incorporate archaeological sites like necropolises, caves, mines, and 'castros' (hillforts). You can also find Romanesque heritage, such as the Church of San Pedro Fiz do Hospital, built with local pink marble. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6971224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil – Triacastela loop from Triacastela&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that connects natural beauty with historical significance.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in O Incio?
A: O Incio's Atlantic climate makes it suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. Winter hikes are possible, especially in the extensive deciduous forests, which offer a different, serene experience.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads in O Incio?
A: As O Incio is a rural municipality, public transport options to specific trailheads might be limited. It is generally advisable to travel by car to access the starting points of many hiking routes. However, some sections of the Camino de Santiago pass through or near O Incio, which may have more accessible transport links.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in O Incio?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, O Incio offers several difficult routes. There are 3 difficult trails available, providing more strenuous climbs and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Street, Sarria – Sculpture loop from A Devesa do Porto&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers a substantial distance.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic scenery along the trails?
A: Yes, given O Incio's varied topography, situated between the Lemos Valley and the Sierra de Courel, many trails offer scenic viewpoints. You can expect vistas over green valleys, gentle mountains, and the tranquil Vilasouto Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sarria – Sculpture loop from Calvor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that provides excellent views of the surrounding landscape.
tips: The church of San Mamede de Vilasouto is located just a few meters from the dammed water of the Vilasouto dam. This old church is of Romanesque origin and is currently unused and is in a state of neglect and progressive deterioration.
The original late Romanesque factory remains in good condition, except at the front, the main entrance and the old bell tower, rebuilt in the mid-18th century.

The Embalse de Vilasouto is a embalse located in the Spanish municipality of Incio, in the province of Lugo. Located between the parishes of Vilasouto, Eirexalba and Goó, it forms part of the basin of the Mao river.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail?
faqs.answer: The Vilasouto Reservoir Trail is rated as moderate. It covers a distance of approximately 14.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 242 meters, typically taking about 4 hours to complete.

faqs.question: Is the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail is designed as a circular route, offering a continuous loop around the Mao River basin and the reservoir.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the San Mamede de Vilasouto Romanesque Church, the Church of Santa María de Goó, and the scenic Vilasouto Reservoir itself.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific details regarding designated parking areas or precise trailhead access points for the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail are not readily available. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival or consulting detailed maps for the most current information.

faqs.question: Is the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash when hiking in natural areas to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Please be mindful of any local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder weather and potentially more challenging trail conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details are not available, as a moderate hike around a reservoir and river basin, you can generally expect a mix of natural paths, possibly some gravel sections, and varied elevation changes typical of a rural landscape.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: Information about specific facilities such as restrooms or designated water sources directly on the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail is not available. It's always best to carry enough water for your hike and plan for restroom breaks before or after your journey.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 14.4 km length, the trail might be challenging for very young children. However, active families used to hiking longer distances could find it enjoyable. Consider the fitness level of all participants before setting out.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Vilasouto Reservoir Trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate, 4-hour hike, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, weather-appropriate clothing (layers are always a good idea), sun protection, and a basic first-aid kit. A map or GPS device is also advisable.
